commit.message:
Fix: Support both old and new metadata schemas in step3

The step2_download_era5_parallel.py script was recently updated to use 'first_time'
and 'last_time' columns instead of 'start_time' and 'end_time' (commit 5f9f8f3).

However, step3_precompute_composites.py was still hardcoded to expect 'start_time',
causing KeyError: 'start_time' when processing data files created with the new
step2 schema while trying to get the case month for climatology lookups.

This fix:
1. Creates _get_case_start_time() helper function to handle both schemas
2. Supports backward compatibility with old metadata files (start_time)
3. Supports new metadata files (first_time)
4. Provides clear error messages if neither column is found

This allows step3 to process mixed old/new metadata files on servers that may
have both legacy and newly-generated data.

commit.message:
Fix PDB Symbol Resolution for Unmerged Windows Traces (#2407)

* Fix PDB symbol resolution for unmerged Windows traces

The ELF symbol work introduced a switch on BinaryFormat in
LookupSymbolsForModule that only handled PE and ELF cases. For
unmerged Windows traces, symbolInfo is null (no RSDS events in the
ETL), so BinaryFormat returns Unspecified, causing symbol resolution
to be skipped entirely.

Add a case for ModuleBinaryFormat.Unspecified that falls back to PDB
lookup on Windows. OpenPdbForModuleFile handles missing PDB signatures
gracefully by checking the local file on disk.

Also rename ModuleBinaryFormat.Unknown to Unspecified to better reflect
that the format was not specified in the trace data.

commit.message:
[build] Use PolySharp for C# 13 support on legacy TFMs (#3642)

[build] Use PolySharp for C# 13 support on legacy TFMs (#3642)

SkiaSharp still ships net462 and netstandard2.x targets, so newer C# language
features need the compiler support attributes that newer frameworks already
provide. Add PolySharp as a private build-time dependency for those legacy
TFMs and update the compatibility package versions used there.

Because PolySharp embeds the required System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis and
System.Runtime.CompilerServices support types into the final assemblies, the
repo-local NullableAttributes.generated.cs shim and its build registration are
no longer needed and can be removed.

Keep the dependency metadata aligned with the final package set by recording the
updated System.Memory and System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe versions in
VERSIONS.txt and the docs dependency scan. Microsoft.Bcl.AsyncInterfaces was
evaluated during the work, but removed after validating that the legacy
SkiaSharp builds still succeed without it.

commit.message:
⚡ Optimize async scraper downloads by offloading blocking I/O (#146)

* perf: offload blocking file writes in scrapers to thread pool

This change optimizes the `download` method in all APK scrapers by
offloading blocking synchronous file I/O operations to a thread pool
using `loop.run_in_executor`. This prevents the main event loop from
lagging during APK downloads, improving overall performance and
responsiveness.

Additionally, a `PermissionError` in `scripts/utils/network.py` occurring
on some filesystems during `chmod` has been mitigated.

Files optimized:
- scripts/scrapers/apkmonk.py
- scripts/scrapers/apkpure.py
- scripts/scrapers/aptoide.py
- scripts/scrapers/archive.py
- scripts/scrapers/uptodown.py
- scripts/utils/network.py (mitigation and async cleanup)

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
nfwd: canonical slot traversal via `_fold_slots` / `_unfold_slots` (#1136)

* Refactor nfwd: canonical slot traversal via _fold_slots / _unfold_slots

Add two traversal helpers to the Nfwd module that define the canonical
slot order exactly once:

- _fold_slots(f, init, x, state): left fold over scalar slots
  (real=1, complex=2 re/im, arrays in eachindex, tuples L→R)
- _unfold_slots(f, x, state): leaf-level structural map over
  primitive leaves (scalars, complex, arrays; tuples recurse)

Refactor existing nfwd machinery to use these helpers:

- _nfwd_input_dof: now a one-liner fold that counts slots
- _nfwd_seed_tangents: tuple recursion replaced by _unfold_slots
- _nfwd_scatter_scalar_chunk: replaces both itself and the recursive
  _nfwd_update_scalar_grad with a single _unfold_slots traversal

Add focused tests verifying the traversal contract: real/complex
scalars, dense real/complex arrays, tuple mixtures, Float32, and
explicit checks that fold and unfold visit slots in the same order.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix nfwd scrutinise findings: docstrings, DOF counting, comment

- Rewrite _fold_slots / _unfold_slots docstrings: each slot is one
  differentiable scalar DOF; complex contributes real-then-imaginary;
  tuples left to right; arrays in eachindex order
- Restore O(1) _nfwd_input_dof specialisations for IEEEFloat, Complex,
  and AbstractArray (fold-based counting was O(n) for arrays)
- Correct section comment: CUDA extension uses _nfwd_leaf_dof /
  _nfwd_slot_meta, not NfwdMooncake

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify: collapse _unfold_slots leaf overloads; fix fragile test

- Collapse four identical _unfold_slots leaf overloads (IEEEFloat,
  Complex, AbstractArray real/complex) into a single Union dispatch;
  same allowlist semantics, no code duplication
- Replace sum_slots test helper with sum_slot_idx: the old helper
  reimplemented _nfwd_add_slot! slot-index conventions (cld, isodd)
  in test code; new helper sums within-leaf slot indices instead,
  testing the same callback contract without the fragile coupling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Drop _nfwd_leaf_dof_type / _nfwd_leaf_dof / _nfwd_slot_meta

These were shims for MooncakeCUDAExt; the CUDA extension will be
refactored to use _fold_slots / _unfold_slots directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor CUDA ext to use _nfwd_input_dof instead of removed helpers

Replace _nfwd_leaf_dof_type, _nfwd_leaf_dof, and _nfwd_slot_meta usage in
MooncakeCUDAExt with _nfwd_type_dof and _nfwd_input_dof via a thin
_gpu_rep_element dispatch that maps broadcast leaf args to representative
scalar elements.

fix: fall back to generic TFLite model when SM8550 model fails

The SM8550-optimised EmbeddingGemma model uses DISPATCH_OP (Qualcomm custom
op) which requires the Qualcomm AI Engine Direct delegate. Without it, the
standard TFLite interpreter throws IllegalStateException on every embed call,
leaving message_embeddings permanently empty.

Try SM8550 model first, catch failure, automatically fall back to the
generic mixed-precision model which runs fine on CPU-only TFLite.

commit.message:
Move `Thread::m_pInterpThreadContext` near top of Thread class for stable asm offset (#126684)

`Thread::m_pInterpThreadContext` was declared at the very end of the
`Thread` class, making its offset highly volatile across build
configurations. Moving it towards the top of the `Thread` class to a
small set of stable, always-present fields at the top of the `Thread`
class will make it a lot more stable, and less prone to build breaks.

Initial repository setup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: repair redirect tester + add GET /api/links/{link_id} endpoint (#19)

## Summary

Fixes the **Redirect tester** feature in the UI, which was silently
broken due to a browser security restriction.

## Root cause

The frontend `testRedirect()` function used `fetch()` with `redirect:
'manual'`. For cross-origin requests (frontend on `:5173`, backend on
`:8000`) this produces an **opaque redirect** response — the browser
hides all headers including `Location`, and `res.status` is always `0`.
As a result, the tester always showed blank status and location.

## Changes

### Backend
- Added `GET /api/links/{link_id}` endpoint that returns `LinkOut`
metadata without triggering a redirect — a clean REST resource read

### Frontend
- `testRedirect()` now calls `GET /api/links/{link_id}` and displays
**redirect code**, **target URL**, and **active** status
- Added `getLink()` helper to `api.ts`

### Tests
- Two new integration tests for the new endpoint (200 with metadata, 404
for unknown ID)
- All 30 tests pass, linting clean

## Also on this branch
- `docs: remove rate limiting requirement` — minor docs update
